memberdef.argsstring: (void)
memberdef.name: ck_get_block_q4_k_size

briefdescription.para: Get Q4_K block size in bytes.

memberdef.argsstring: (void)
memberdef.name: ck_get_block_q6_k_size

briefdescription.para: Get Q6_K block size in bytes.

memberdef.argsstring: (void)
memberdef.name: ck_get_block_q8_k_size

briefdescription.para: Get Q8_K block size in bytes.

memberdef.argsstring: (void)
memberdef.name: ck_get_qk_k

briefdescription.para: Get QK_K (elements per super-block)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *src, float *dst, int n)
memberdef.name: ck_test_dequant_q4_0

briefdescription.para: Dequantize Q4_0 data to FP32.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *src, float *dst, int n)
memberdef.name: ck_test_dequant_q4_k

briefdescription.para: Dequantize Q4_K data to FP32.

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements (must be multiple of 256)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *src, float *dst, int n)
memberdef.name: ck_test_dequant_q6_k

briefdescription.para: Dequantize Q6_K data to FP32.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q4k, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols, int n_tokens)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemm_q4_k

briefdescription.para: Q4_K GEMM - batched matrix multiply with quantized weights.

detaileddescription.para: Computes: output[t,r] = sum_k(weight[r,k] * input[t,k])

parameterdescription.para: Number of columns (must be multiple of 256)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q5_0, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols, int n_tokens)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemm_q5_0

briefdescription.para: Q5_0 GEMM - batched matrix multiply with Q5_0 weights (32-element blocks)

detaileddescription.para: Computes: output[t,r] = sum_k(weight[r,k] * input[t,k])

weight_q5_0
Q5_0 quantized weights [rows, cols]
input_f32
FP32 input [n_tokens, cols]
output
FP32 output [n_tokens, rows]
rows
Number of output rows
cols
Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)
n_tokens
Batch size
Q5_0 GEMM - batched matrix multiply with Q5_0 weights (32-element blocks)
Used for MLP W1 (gate/up projection) and attention Q/K with Q5_0 weights.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q6k, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols, int n_tokens)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemm_q6_k

briefdescription.para: Q6_K GEMM - batched matrix multiply with Q6_K weights.

detaileddescription.para: Computes: output[t,r] = sum_k(weight[r,k] * input[t,k])

weight_q6k
Q6_K quantized weights [rows, cols]
input_f32
FP32 input [n_tokens, cols]
output
FP32 output [n_tokens, rows]
rows
Number of output rows
cols
Number of columns (must be multiple of 256)
n_tokens
Batch size
Q6_K GEMM - batched matrix multiply with Q6_K weights.
Used for MLP W2 (down projection) with Q6_K weights.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q8_0, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols, int n_tokens)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemm_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Q8_0 GEMM - batched matrix multiply with Q8_0 weights (32-element blocks)

detaileddescription.para: Computes: output[t,r] = sum_k(weight[r,k] * input[t,k])

weight_q8_0
Q8_0 quantized weights [rows, cols]
input_f32
FP32 input [n_tokens, cols]
output
FP32 output [n_tokens, rows]
rows
Number of output rows
cols
Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)
n_tokens
Batch size
Q8_0 GEMM - batched matrix multiply with Q8_0 weights (32-element blocks)
Used for attention V projection with Q8_0 weights.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q4k, const float *input_f32, float *output, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemv_q4_k

briefdescription.para: Q4_K GEMV - dot product of quantized weights and FP32 input.

detaileddescription.para: Internally quantizes input to Q8_K, then computes dot product.

parameterdescription.para: Number of columns (must be multiple of 256)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q5_0, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemv_q5_0

briefdescription.para: Q5_0 GEMV - matrix-vector multiply with Q5_0 weights.

parameterdescription.para: Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q5_0, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemv_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Q5_0 x Q8_0 quantized GEMV - matches llama.cpp's approach.

detaileddescription.para: This version quantizes the input to Q8_0 first, then uses integer dot products (like llama.cpp does). Use this for parity testing.

parameterdescription.para: FP32 input vector [cols] - will be quantized to Q8_0
Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q6k, const float *input_f32, float *output, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemv_q6_k

briefdescription.para: Q6_K GEMV.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q8_0, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemv_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Q8_0 GEMV - matrix-vector multiply with Q8_0 weights.

parameterdescription.para: Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q8_0, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemv_q8_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Q8_0 x Q8_0 quantized GEMV - matches llama.cpp's approach.

detaileddescription.para: This version quantizes the input to Q8_0 first, then uses integer dot products (like llama.cpp does). Use this for parity testing.

parameterdescription.para: FP32 input vector [cols] - will be quantized to Q8_0
Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *src, void *dst, int n)
memberdef.name: ck_test_quantize_q8_k

briefdescription.para: Quantize FP32 to Q8_K (for activations)

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements (must be multiple of 256)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *weight, float *output, int n_tokens, int dim, float eps)
memberdef.name: ck_test_rmsnorm

briefdescription.para: RMSNorm.

detaileddescription.para: Computes: output = (input / rms(input)) * weight where rms(x) = sqrt(mean(x^2) + eps)

memberdef.argsstring: (float *q, float *k, int n_tokens, int n_heads, int n_heads_kv, int head_dim, int pos_offset, float theta)
memberdef.name: ck_test_rope

briefdescription.para: RoPE (Rotary Position Embedding)

detaileddescription.para: Applies rotary position embeddings to Q and K tensors.
NOTE: CK uses rotate-half format (split first/second halves) while some implementations use interleaved format. The test harness should account for this.

parameterdescription.para: Query tensor [n_tokens, n_heads * head_dim], modified in-place
Key tensor [n_tokens, n_heads_kv * head_dim], modified in-place

memberdef.argsstring: (float *q, float *k, int n_tokens, int n_heads, int n_heads_kv, int head_dim, int pos_offset, float theta)
memberdef.name: ck_test_rope_interleaved

briefdescription.para: RoPE with interleaved format (for llama.cpp compatibility)

detaileddescription.para: Uses interleaved format: (x0, x1) -> (x0*cos - x1*sin, x0*sin + x1*cos)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, float *output, int n)
memberdef.name: ck_test_softmax

briefdescription.para: Softmax (simple, non-causal)

detaileddescription.para: Computes: output[i] = exp(input[i]) / sum(exp(input))

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *gate_up, float *output, int n_tokens, int intermediate_dim)
memberdef.name: ck_test_swiglu

briefdescription.para: SwiGLU activation.

detaileddescription.para: Computes: output = SiLU(gate) * up where SiLU(x) = x * sigmoid(x)

parameterdescription.para: Input tensor [n_tokens, 2 * intermediate_dim] Layout: [gate_0..gate_D-1, up_0..up_D-1] per token

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q5_0, const void *input_q8_0, float *output, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_vec_dot_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Direct Q5_0 x Q8_0 dot product (takes pre-quantized Q8_0 input)

detaileddescription.para: This is a "direct" test that bypasses FP32-to-Q8_0 conversion. Useful for isolating kernel bugs from quantization bugs.

weight_q5_0
Q5_0 quantized weights [cols]
input_q8_0
Q8_0 quantized input [cols] (pre-quantized!)
output
Output scalar [1]
cols
Number of elements (must be multiple of 32)
Direct Q5_0 x Q8_0 dot product (takes pre-quantized Q8_0 input)
This is a "direct" test that bypasses FP32-to-Q8_0 conversion. Useful for isolating kernel bugs from quantization bugs.

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q8_0, const void *input_q8_0, float *output, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_vec_dot_q8_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Direct Q8_0 x Q8_0 dot product (takes pre-quantized Q8_0 input)

detaileddescription.para:
weight_q8_0
Q8_0 quantized weights [cols]
input_q8_0
Q8_0 quantized input [cols] (pre-quantized!)
output
Output scalar [1]
cols
Number of elements (must be multiple of 32)
Direct Q8_0 x Q8_0 dot product (takes pre-quantized Q8_0 input)

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements (must be multiple of 32)

briefdescription.para: C-Kernel-Engine Parity Testing API.

detaileddescription.para: Exposes individual CK kernels for parity testing against llama.cpp/ggml. This API mirrors the test-kernel-parity.cpp interface in llama.cpp.

Usage:
Build as shared library: libck_parity.so
Load from Python using ctypes
Call functions with matching signatures to test-kernel-parity.cpp
